question.label: Growth blockers
question.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
question.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

answer: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.

assessment.note: He names a concrete growth blocker — being pulled into execution details despite wanting bigger scope — which signals strong growth constraint with some self-awareness about the gap.

new_queue.label: Execution details
new_queue.name: Execution details — where are you still getting pulled in most often?

new_queue.label: Stretch now
new_queue.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?

new_queue.label: Scope definition
new_queue.name: When you think about the next-level Head of Product role, what does good actually look like to you?

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Influence target
new_queue.name: Which stakeholder forum matters most for your influence at this level?

new_queue.label: Tradeoff ask
new_queue.name: What would you stop doing if you wanted to spend more time on strategic ownership?

new_queue.label: Manager leverage
new_queue.name: What should I be doing more of to help you build that scope?

new_queue.label: First move
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to start first?

history.answer_excerpt: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
